title: Avoid hardcoded credentials
description: Hardcoded credentials in source code create serious security vulnerabilities by exposing sensitive authentication information. These credentials can be discovered by anyone with access to the code, leading to unauthorized system access.
recommendation: Store credentials in configuration files, environment variables, or secure credential management systems.

noncompliant.description: Hardcoded credentials expose sensitive authentication information in source code.

compliant.description: Use environment variables, configuration files, or secure credential management systems instead of hardcoding.

checks.message: Store credentials in configuration files, environment variables, or secure credential management systems.
Store credentials in configuration files, environment variables, or secure credential management systems.
